scope: Low-risk launch performance fixes from data/launch-performance-audit-report.json

summary.browserSmokeStatus: Acrylia Real Spawn page loaded with Real Spawn Intelligence and Spawn Map visible; no console errors were reported.

changesMade.change: Stopped loading full per-zone EQEmu loot JSON in app/zones/[slug]/page.tsx. The dynamic zone route now loads summary/spawn data only for Real Spawn mode.
changesMade.impact: Avoids serializing very large loot/debug payloads into the initial server-rendered zone response.

changesMade.change: Added scripts/generate-real-spawn-loot-assets.mjs and generated public/real-spawn-loot-assets/{zone}.json files with itemDetails stripped out.
changesMade.impact: Drop rows still get item names, chances, icons, and loot table context from cache-friendly static JSON, while large EQEmu item detail blobs are removed from the zone loot payload.

changesMade.change: Generated public/real-spawn-item-assets/{itemId}.json fallback item detail files and lazy-load them from drop rows on hover/click when richer existing item data is unavailable.
changesMade.impact: Heavy EQEmu fallback item stats are deferred until the user inspects an item.

changesMade.change: Removed the eager client import of data/eqemu-native-map-debug-index.json from ZoneDetailExplorer. Spawn-map data now derives minimal spawn point data from the already loaded Real Spawn spawn rows and uses the small zone-map asset manifest.
changesMade.impact: Avoids bundling a multi-megabyte debug index into the zone detail client component.

changesMade.change: Kept map geometry in public/zone-map-assets and added IntersectionObserver-based loading so the per-zone map asset is fetched only when the map section is near the viewport.
changesMade.impact: Preserves maps while deferring dot/geometry-heavy payloads until they are actually needed.

changesMade.change: Throttled item preview mousemove updates so the tooltip does not rerender on tiny cursor movements across dense drop rows.
changesMade.impact: Reduces client-side render churn without changing hover/click behavior.

changesMade.change: Changed /api/status from revalidate=0 to revalidate=60 and updated the route comment.
changesMade.impact: Reduces Function Invocation risk from public status checks while keeping status fresh enough for launch use.

changesMade.change: Reviewed usage. The client loads reports once on Bonus page mount and on explicit user actions; no automatic polling loop was found.
changesMade.impact: No code change made. Route remains no-store because report mutations/admin state need fresh data, but it is not currently poll-driven.

eqemuItemDetailAssets.loadingBehavior: Fetched on demand from drop row hover/click for EQEmu fallback details.

mapGeometryAssets.loadingBehavior: Fetched client-side only when the map section intersects or nears the viewport.
mapGeometryAssets.debugIndexStatus: data/eqemu-native-map-debug-index.json is no longer imported by ZoneDetailExplorer.

/api/bonus/reports.riskAfter: Moderate for admin/report-heavy use. Keep watching during launch, but no broad rewrite was made.

staticZoneData.status: Real Spawn loot and map assets use public static JSON instead of new server/API calls.

clientSideFilteringStatus./zones: Search, expansion, level, race, and sort filters remain client-side after initial data load.
clientSideFilteringStatus./zones/[slug] Real Spawn: Real Spawn filters/search/sort remain client-side after spawn data and static loot asset load.

remainingRisks.risk: Some stripped Real Spawn loot assets are still several MB for dense zones.
remainingRisks.suggestedNextStep: Split large loot assets by mob initial, npcTypeId, or only fetch drops for expanded rows.

remainingRisks.risk: Existing item-details.json is still imported by several client components, including ZoneDetailExplorer for rich item matching.
remainingRisks.suggestedNextStep: Shard existing item details into an item lookup service/static chunk loaded on hover or route demand.
Convert the largest PNGs to WebP/AVIF and use responsive image loading.

remainingRisks.risk: SVG map rendering can still be DOM-heavy for large zones.
remainingRisks.suggestedNextStep: Consider canvas rendering or per-layer geometry simplification after launch.
Add short-lived server caching or stricter pagination if report traffic rises.
